decks.notes: This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes support cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to deal direct damage and control the board. Seasoned Pyromancer helps generate card advantage, while cards like Thraben Charm and Voice of Victory provide additional combat tricks and removal options. The inclusion of cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison further disrupt the opponent's game plan, setting up for a powerful aggressive strategy.

The main concept of this Modern UR Aggro deck is to quickly establish board presence and apply pressure to the opponent through aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Slickshot Show-Off, while using spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to control the board and deal direct damage. The deck aims to be fast and efficient, utilizing cards like Mishra's Bauble and Cori-Steel Cutter to maintain card advantage and tempo. By combining efficient creatures and burn spells, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The key concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, aiming to deal damage quickly and efficiently. By utilizing cheap spells like Lightning Bolt, Lava Spike, and Skewer the Critics, along with powerful creatures like Goblin Guide and Monastery Swiftspear, the goal is to apply constant pressure to the opponent while also having the flexibility to finish them off with burn spells like Boros Charm and Lightning Helix. The mana base is designed to support this strategy by providing consistent access to all the necessary colors of mana.

The main concept of this Aggro-Affinity deck is to quickly flood the board with artifact creatures and utilize cards like Mishra's Bauble and Preordain to draw into key pieces. The deck takes advantage of cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to generate mana and create powerful artifacts. With efficient removal like Engineered Explosives and Kappa Cannoneer, the deck aims to control the board while continuously pressuring the opponent. The inclusion of Claws of Gix and Pithing Needle allows for disruption of the opponent's game plan, while cards like Shadowspear provide additional utility and evasion for the creatures.

The deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures and utilizing various spells and abilities to deal damage quickly and efficiently to the opponent. It combines the colors of red and white, utilizing cards that boost creature power and provide removal options. The deck includes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with support cards like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless assault, while also providing some control elements to handle opposing threats effectively.

The main theme of this Boros Aggro deck is to maintain board control through aggressive creature plays and direct damage spells while utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to boost creature strength and provide additional support. The deck focuses on quick and efficient plays, using cards like Guide of Souls and Ranger-Captain of Eos to maintain a steady stream of threats while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison. The inclusion of versatile spells like Mana Tithe and Lightning Bolt adds an element of surprise and flexibility to the deck's gameplay.

The main idea of this Ruby Storm deck is to quickly generate mana and card advantage through spells like Manamorphose and Desperate Ritual, while utilizing Ral, Monsoon Mage and other spell payoff cards to deal damage to the opponent. The deck aims to create explosive turns where multiple spells are cast in a single turn to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells. The combo potential of the deck is maximized through the use of cards like Past in Flames and Wish, allowing the player to dig for key pieces and win the game in a single turn.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost threats like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, backed up by burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to pressure the opponent and finish them off before they have time to stabilize. Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration help to dig for more threats and keep the aggression going, while cards like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge can help push through damage or protect key creatures. The deck aims to be fast, efficient, and relentless in its pursuit of victory.

This Modern Amulet Titan deck focuses on utilizing Amulet of Vigor to generate additional land drops and mana acceleration, allowing for explosive plays with Primeval Titan and Scapeshift to quickly assemble a lethal combo. The deck also includes utility lands like Urza's Saga and Tolaria West to search for key pieces, while cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Summoner's Pact provide additional consistency in finding important creatures. The deck aims to ramp up quickly, control the board, and generate a game-winning combo as efficiently as possible.

The deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures using cards like Faithless Looting and Goryo's Vengeance, and then reanimate them at instant speed. Atraxa, Grand Unifier provides additional value by boosting the reanimated creatures. Consign to Memory and Fatal Push help control the board while Thoughtseize disrupts opponents' plans. Griselbrand and Ulamog, the Defiler are key targets for reanimation, while Force of Negation and Prismari Charm provide protection and versatility. Psychic Frog and Emperor of Bones offer additional reanimation targets to overwhelm opponents.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and creature control. By utilizing cards like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Snapcaster Mage, the deck aims to prevent the opponent from executing their strategy effectively. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Field of Ruin and Undercity Sewers to control the opponent's resources and Orcish Bowmasters to deal with threats on the board. With a strong emphasis on disruption and control, this deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition.

The key concept of this UW Control deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal, and bounce spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to stall the game until it can establish a strong board presence with planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. It also utilizes card advantage with cards like Meticulous Archive and Monumental Henge to outvalue the opponent. The deck's win condition usually comes from either overwhelming the opponent with card advantage or ultimating its planeswalkers for a game-ending advantage.

The deck focuses on combining aggressive creatures with blink effects to control the battlefield and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler, the deck aims to repeatedly exile and return key creatures like Witch Enchanter and Overlord of the Balemurk to generate card advantage and maintain pressure on the opponent. With a mix of removal spells like Fatal Push and powerful finishers like Solitude and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and disruption.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with interactive spells like Force of Negation and Thoughtseize. A key aspect of the deck is the use of Ephemerate to repeatedly reanimate creatures, generating value and keeping up pressure. A strong synergy is also present with cards like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Quantum Riddler, allowing for additional card advantage and control over the game. The goal is to outpace and overwhelm the opponent with efficient combos and disruptive plays.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to apply early pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer allows for additional card advantage and board control. The deck also utilizes cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to deal direct damage to the opponent's life total while maintaining board presence. The combination of efficient creatures, burn spells, and versatile planeswalkers makes this deck a formidable force in the Modern format.

The main focus of this deck is to be aggressive and put pressure on the opponent early on. The deck utilizes efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Nethergoyf, along with spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to quickly reduce the opponent's life total. The deck also includes cards like Mishra's Bauble and Faithless Looting for card advantage and to help set up combos. Overall, the Rakdos Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, powerful creatures and burn spells to secure a quick victory.
